name: Salicylic Acid
description: Salicylic acid is naturally present in many fruits or certain plants such as willow bark. It is used in cosmetics as a preservative, hair conditioner or for its smoothing and regenerating effects. Salicylic acid is also often used as a keratolytic in anti-dandruff shampoos, particularly for the treatment of oily dandruff (steatoid pityriasis).In an opinion published in September 2018, the European Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety (SCCS) concludes that salicylic acid is not a problem if used under the regulatory conditions (see below), however it states that: 'This opinion does not apply to products that are administered orally such as toothpastes and mouthwashes. Products to be sprayed that could result in exposure of the consumer's lung by inhalation are also excluded". Salicylic acid is allowed in Organic.Salicylic acid is classified CMR category 2: Suspected CMR effect, but insufficient information is available.* CMR means Carcinogenic, Mutagenic and Reprotoxic
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Natural.

However, this dose can be bypassed if the product is a medicine and is subject to a Marketing Authorization (MA), in which case it can only be purchased in pharmacies.

name: Sodium Hyaluronate
description: Sodium hyaluronate is the salt of hyaluronic acid. This active ingredient is appreciated for its moisturizing and plumping effect on the skin. It is a natural component of the connective tissue. It easily penetrates the epidermis and is then able to fix a large amount of water, up to 20 times its weight. For this reason, it is found in many cosmetics such as anti-aging, moisturizing creams or even foundations ...Discovered in the 1930s, hyaluronic acid is abundant in the dermis of young skin, but its quantity decreases with age: the skin appears more damaged, with wrinkles. Hyaluronic acid is a fairly large molecule that has difficulty penetrating the skin, and is much more effective injection. In a cosmetic, it is its salt that is used: sodium hyaluronate is a much smaller molecule, which penetrates more easily and can then move into the deep layers of the skin.This salt is a powerful humectant that attracts and holds water to it, making it the moisturizer of choice for the skin: it helps keep it "wet". As it attracts and binds to the water, it causes a slight swelling which reduces the appearance of lines and wrinkles, to give the skin a younger appearance.It is authorized in Bio.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Jojobate

name: Sodium Lactate
description: Sodium lactate is the sodium salt of lactic acid, a type of alpha-hydroxy acid (AHA). It is produced by the fermentation of sugars, e.g. from corn or sugar beet. It contains no milk proteins. It can therefore be used by people with a milk allergy or lactose intolerance. It is used as a food additive under the name E325. It is used in cosmetics for its antioxidant, antimicrobial and moisturizing properties.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate
description: Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate or SCI is a mild anionic surfactant without sulfate. Derived from the fatty acids of coconut oil and isethionic acid, it has a very good foaming power. It is found in natural products such as solid shampoos.SCI is produced by ethoxylation, which makes it a not very ecological ingredient. It is prohibited in the COSMOS specifications.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Vegetal.

name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
description: Sodium Laureth Sulphate (SLES) is an anionic surfactant used in cosmetics for its detergent properties. It is therefore very present in shower gels and shampoos. It is produced from Sodium Lauryl Sulphate (SLS) by ethoxylation, a very polluting chemical transformation, which is why it is prohibited in organic products. Sodium Laureth Sulphate is reputed to be less irritating than Sodium Lauryl Sulphate although the latter is more natural and authorized in Organic, it is the chemical transformation that has softened it and increased its foaming power.This ingredient is classified as ecotoxic according to REACH (Aquatic Chronic 3).
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sodium Hydroxymethylglycinate
description: Sodium hydroxymethylglycinate is used as a preservative in cosmetics as an alternative to parabens. It acts on its own on a fairly broad spectrum of microbes and bacteria of all kinds. Nevertheless, some studies show that it can release formaldehyde (known carcinogen, irritant and allergen) under certain conditions which, let's face it, seem infrequent. Moreover, when this is the case, the quantities released are negligible. However, considering that it is possible to do otherwise, we classify this ingredient in red on Inci Beauty. The ingredient is reputed to be very irritating to the skin and eyes, and seems to give off a very strong rancid odor even below the concentrations authorized by Europe. This leads manufacturers to dilute it too much and therefore reduce its preservative effect or to use too many masking agents.
note: Рекомендуется избегать в косметических продуктах. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

Unlike SCI (Sodium Cocoyl Isethionate), which allows for the creation of opaque products, SLMI has excellent water solubility and therefore allows for the creation of sulfate-free, transparent shampoo formulations. It is often used in conjunction with CAPB.SLMI is produced by ethoxylation, which makes it an environmentally unfriendly ingredient. It is prohibited in the COSMOS specifications.

It is used as a replacement for Sodium Fluoride, however, no study today can prove increased effectiveness compared to the latter.The fluoride concentration should not exceed 1500 ppm (F) in a "cosmetic" type toothpaste. However, this dose can be circumvented if the product is a medicine and is the subject of a marketing authorization (MA), in which case it can only be purchased in pharmacies.

description: Sodium Lauryl sulfate is a surfactant widely used in cosmetics. It is produced mostly from palm oil. It has a very strong foaming and detergent power, but it is criticized for its very irritating and drying effect on the skin, so much so that it is found less and less often in shower gels and bath products. Manufacturers prefer to use the ethoxylated version (SLES = Sodium Laureth Sulfate) less abrasive or other softer surfactants. However, it remains very present in toothpastes for adults (57% contain it) and children (42%).SLS is also criticized for being a pollutant for the environment, animals, aquatic environments or even plants. Nevertheless, it is authorized in organic.It is classified according to REACH: Ecotoxic (Aquatic Chronic 3).

It is quite frequently found for the same reasons in moisturizing creams. In food, it is found as an additive under the name E420 or as a sweetener for diabetics.Industrially, Sorbitol is obtained, for example, by the hydrogenation of corn or glucose syrup. If it's not clear, call the brand to confirm. Squalane is produced by hydrogenation of squalene.

description: Squalene oil can be of vegetable (olive oil), animal (shark liver oil) or synthetic origin. Controversies exist when squalene is of animal origin, and extracted from the liver of sharks: the animal is killed, its viscera are extracted and its carcass is thrown back into the sea.When it is of plant origin, squalene can be called phytosqualene. Make sure it is of vegetable origin in your products, do not hesitate to contact the brand to make sure. However, most squalene used today is no longer of animal origin.Squalene is used primarily as an emollient in many creams. Squalane is a purified and hydrogenated form of squalene, much more used in cosmetics.

name: Styrene/Acrylates Copolymer
description: Styrene/acrylates copolymer is a synthetic polymer used as a film-forming agent in cosmetics: it forms a film on the skin which does not penetrate it. It is used in many formulations for its color and covering power.
note: Требует осторожности при использовании. Ингредиент регулируется в ЕС и Великобритании. Происхождение: Synthetic.

name: Sucralose
description: Sucralose is an artificial sweetener with a sweetening power 600 times greater than that of sugar. It is used in cosmetics mainly in mouthwashes (some toothpastes also contain it), because it is non-cariogenic. In food, you will find it under the name E955 (Canderel), in very many "sweet" processed products.
note: Считается безопасным для использования в косметике. Происхождение: Synthetic.
